name: align_exceeds_input
code:
proof fn phi_1_align_exceeds_input(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) > a,
{
}
reason: Aligning down should never produce a value strictly greater than the original input

name: align_result_not_multiple
code:
proof fn phi_2_align_result_not_multiple(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) % b != 0,
{
}
reason: The result of aligning to b should always be a multiple of b; failing this means the function is broken

name: aligned_identity_disagree
code:
proof fn phi_3_aligned_identity_disagree(addr: usize, size: usize)
    requires
        size > 0,
        addr % size == 0,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(addr, size) != addr,
{
}
reason: If addr is already aligned to size, align_to_usize should be the identity; disagreement indicates inconsistency

name: not_aligned_to_one
code:
proof fn phi_4_not_aligned_to_one(addr: nat)
    requires
        true,
    ensures
        !aligned(addr, 1),
{
}
reason: Every natural number is aligned to 1; if this verifies, the aligned spec is vacuously broken

name: align_always_zero
code:
proof fn phi_5_align_always_zero(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
        a > 0,
        a >= b,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) == 0,
{
}
reason: For a >= b > 0, the aligned-down result should be at least b, not zero; verifying this signals a degenerate spec
